spec.name: vol_filtered_momentum
spec.description: 低ボラ環境でのみモメンタムに乗る
spec.code:
import pandas as pd

def generate_signals(df):
    ret5 = df["Close"].pct_change(5)
    vol20 = df["Close"].pct_change().rolling(20).std()
    sig = pd.Series(0, index=df.index)
    sig[(ret5 > 0) & (vol20 < vol20.rolling(60).median())] = 1
    sig[(ret5 < 0) & (vol20 > vol20.rolling(60).median())] = -1
    return sig

spec.name: adaptive_sma
spec.description: SMA crossover where windows are adjusted based on recent volatility to reduce whipsaws.
spec.code:
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

def generate_signals(df):
    prices = df['close'].values
    returns = np.diff(prices) / prices[:-1]
    vol = pd.Series(returns).rolling(window=20).std().values
    vol = np.append(np.nan, vol)  # align length
    # base windows
    base_fast = 10
    base_slow = 30
    # adjust: increase windows when volatility is high
    vol_ratio = np.nan_to_num(vol / np.nanmean(vol), nan=1.0)
    fast_window = (base_fast * (1 + vol_ratio)).astype(int)
    slow_window = (base_slow * (1 + vol_ratio)).astype(int)
    # cap windows to max 99 and min 5
    fast_window = np.clip(fast_window, 5, 29)
    slow_window = np.clip(slow_window, 20, 99)
    # compute SMA
    sma_fast = pd.Series(prices).rolling(window=int(np.nanmean(fast_window))).mean()
    sma_slow = pd.Series(prices).rolling(window=int(np.nanmean(slow_window))).mean()
    signal = np.where(sma_fast > sma_slow, 1, -1)
    return signal

source.ref: RSI mean reversion on AMZN with moderate thresholds

spec.name: atr_channel_breakout
spec.description: Breakout strategy using ATR-based dynamic channels
spec.code:
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

def generate_signals(df):
    period = 20
    multiplier = 2.0
    high = df['High']
    low = df['Low']
    close = df['Close']
    tr1 = high - low
    tr2 = (high - close.shift()).abs()
    tr3 = (low - close.shift()).abs()
    tr = pd.concat([tr1, tr2, tr3], axis=1).max(axis=1)
    atr = tr.rolling(period).mean()
    mid = close.rolling(period).mean()
    upper = mid + multiplier * atr
    lower = mid - multiplier * atr
    signal = pd.Series(0, index=df.index)
    signal[close > upper] = 1
    signal[close < lower] = -1
    return signal

spec.name: dual_hold_momentum_googl
spec.description: Momentum with dual lookback periods on GOOGL. Combines short and long lookback momentum signals to filter noise and capture sustained trends. Signal is 1 when both lookbacks are positive, -1 when both negative, 0 otherwise.
spec.code:
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

def generate_signals(df):
    """
    Dual momentum: combines short-term (10-day) and long-term (40-day) momentum.
    Signal = 1 if both positive, -1 if both negative, 0 else.
    """
    # Price series
    close = df['Close']
    # Short-term ROC
    roc_short = close.pct_change(periods=10)
    # Long-term ROC
    roc_long = close.pct_change(periods=40)
    # Signs
    sign_short = np.sign(roc_short)
    sign_long = np.sign(roc_long)
    # Combined signal: require both same direction
    signal = np.where((sign_short == 1) & (sign_long == 1), 1,
                     np.where((sign_short == -1) & (sign_long == -1), -1, 0))
    signal = pd.Series(signal, index=df.index)
    # Fill initial NaN with 0
    signal = signal.fillna(0).astype(int)
    return signal

spec.name: volatility_adaptive_sma_btc
spec.description: SMA crossover with windows adapted to Bitcoin volatility. Uses shorter windows in high volatility regimes and longer windows in low volatility regimes to reduce whipsaws.
spec.code:
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

def generate_signals(df):
    """
    Volatility-adaptive SMA crossover for BTC-USD.
    Regime determined by ATR relative to its median: high vol -> fast (5,20), low vol -> slow (20,50).
    """
    high = df['High']
    low = df['Low']
    close = df['Close']
    # ATR (14-day)
    tr = pd.DataFrame({
        'h_l': high - low,
        'h_c_prev': (high - close.shift(1)).abs(),
        'l_c_prev': (low - close.shift(1)).abs()
    }).max(axis=1)
    atr = tr.rolling(14).mean()
    # Median ATR over last 60 days
    median_atr = atr.rolling(60).median()
    # Regime: high volatility if atr > 1.2 * median_atr
    regime_high = atr > (1.2 * median_atr)
    # Compute regime-dependent SMAs
    fast_high = close.rolling(5).mean()
    slow_high = close.rolling(20).mean()
    fast_low = close.rolling(20).mean()
    slow_low = close.rolling(50).mean()
    # Select SMA pair based on regime
    fast_sma = np.where(regime_high, fast_high, fast_low)
    slow_sma = np.where(regime_high, slow_high, slow_low)
    # Signal
    signal = np.where(fast_sma > slow_sma, 1, np.where(fast_sma < slow_sma, -1, 0))
    signal = pd.Series(signal, index=df.index)
    signal = signal.fillna(0).astype(int)
    return signal

spec.name: vol_price_breakout
spec.description: Volume-confirmed price direction: long on up day with above-average volume, short on down day with above-average volume
spec.code:
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

def generate_signals(df):
    vol_ma = df["Volume"].rolling(20).mean()
    up = df["Close"] > df["Open"]
    down = df["Close"] < df["Open"]
    signals = pd.Series(0, index=df.index)
    signals[up & (df["Volume"] > vol_ma)] = 1
    signals[down & (df["Volume"] > vol_ma)] = -1
    return signals

spec.name: hybrid_momentum_meanrev
spec.description: Hybrid strategy combining momentum (lookback 30) and mean reversion (threshold 2.0) for AMZN. Long when price is oversold (z-score < -2.0) and momentum is positive; short when overbought and momentum is negative.
spec.code:
import pandas as pd

def generate_signals(df):
    lookback = 30
    threshold = 2.0
    close = df['Close']
    mom = close.pct_change(lookback)
    rolling_mean = close.rolling(window=lookback).mean()
    rolling_std = close.rolling(window=lookback).std()
    zscore = (close - rolling_mean) / rolling_std
    signal = pd.Series(0, index=df.index)
    signal[(zscore < -threshold) & (mom > 0)] = 1
    signal[(zscore > threshold) & (mom < 0)] = -1
    return signal
